rules.name: Material graph node count must stay within budget
rules.message: Material shader graph has more nodes than the configured budget.
rules.why: Large shader graphs are harder to review, slower to evaluate, and more likely to hide expensive or broken branches. Budget checks keep material networks maintainable before publish.

rules.name: Material texture count must stay within budget
rules.message: Material uses more texture nodes than the configured budget.
rules.why: Excessive texture count increases memory pressure, IO cost, review complexity, and farm risk. Keeping texture count within budget helps maintain predictable rendering cost.

rules.name: Material graph depth must stay within budget
rules.message: Material shader graph is deeper than the configured budget.
rules.why: Deep shader graphs are costly to inspect and can signal overly complex procedural or layered networks. Depth budgets help identify networks that need TD review.

rules.name: Material expensive node count must stay within budget
rules.message: Material uses more high-cost shader nodes than the configured budget.
rules.why: Layered, blend, and SSS shader nodes increase shading cost disproportionately. Counting expensive node types helps flag materials that may slow farm renders.

rules.name: Material farm cost score must stay within budget
rules.message: Material weighted farm cost score exceeds the configured budget.
rules.why: Weighted graph cost estimates aggregate node-type render expense. Materials above the budget are more likely to increase farm time and memory pressure.
